name: Grass-Fed Beef Tallow
description: Rendered fat from grass-fed beef suet whose fatty acid profile closely mirrors human sebum, rich in fat-soluble vitamins A, D, E, and K.
chemicalName: Mixed triglycerides of stearic, oleic, and palmitic acids
appearance: White to pale yellow solid at room temperature; liquid when melted
solubility: Insoluble in water; soluble in ether, chloroform, warm ethanol
chemistry: Fatty acids mirror human sebum (~45% oleic, ~25% palmitic). Vitamins A, D, E, K. Grass-fed has 4x more vitamin E + CLA (anti-inflammatory).
warnings: Toxins concentrate in fat — grass-fed only. Faint smell if not purified.
